diagrams.title: Three Basic 2D Transformations
diagrams.description: Shows a house-shaped polygon undergoing the three fundamental geometric transformations: translation (moving), rotation (turning), and scaling (resizing). The original shape is shown in blue, and each transformed version in a different color. This visual comparison helps students understand how each transformation affects the shape's position, orientation, and size independently.
elements: translated shape (green, moved right and up)
diagrams.purpose: Introduces and differentiates the three fundamental 2D transformation types: translation, rotation, and scaling

diagrams.title: Point Rotation Around Origin
diagrams.description: Demonstrates rotating a point P(x, y) around the origin by an angle θ. Shows the original point, the rotated point P', the angle arc, and the radius line. Displays the mathematical formulas for rotation: x' = x·cos(θ) - y·sin(θ) and y' = x·sin(θ) + y·cos(θ). This helps students visualize the trigonometric relationship between the original and rotated coordinates.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the rotation transformation formula and its geometric interpretation around the origin

diagrams.title: Composite Transformation Sequence
diagrams.description: Illustrates a composite transformation showing a shape being translated, then rotated, then scaled. Displays the corresponding transformation matrices and shows how matrix multiplication combines them into a single transformation matrix. The step-by-step visualization helps students understand how complex transformations are built from simple ones.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how multiple transformations are combined through matrix multiplication to create composite transformations
